title: Construct EVM bytecode in memory at runtime for single-use contract deployment
description: Constructs minimal EVM bytecode directly in memory using mstore/mstore8, then deploys it with create(). The bytecode is assembled byte-by-byte: mstore8(offset, 0x73) writes the PUSH20 opcode, the target address is already in memory from a previous mstore, and mstore8(offset, 0xff) writes SELFDESTRUCT. The result is a 22-byte init code that executes immediately upon deployment. No Solidity contract compilation needed.
pattern_after:
mstore(0x00, to)
mstore8(0x0b, 0x73) // PUSH20 opcode
mstore8(0x20, 0xff) // SELFDESTRUCT opcode
create(amount, 0x0b, 0x16) // deploy 22-byte contract
when_to_apply: Single-use contracts like force-send ETH or CREATE2 address mining
when_not_to_apply: Contracts that need to persist beyond the deployment transaction
